suggestion_reasons.Design Rationale with Named Alternatives: The user explicitly said this is what C did better than A and B — 'framing of what was rejected and why is exactly the kind of context that actually teaches' — but then said C was unfinished and A came close. The rubric is capturing this well enough that the rubric-guided draft scored highest; kept unchanged.
suggestion_reasons.Warning and Gotcha Placement: The user's core complaint about the rubric-guided draft was that the 'synchronous costume' heuristic 'lands as a quip when it should land as a warning, and warnings should hit before you're in the situation, not as a retrospective observation' — which is exactly what this criterion requires. The rubric is right; the execution drifted. Added a dimension explicitly prohibiting retrospective framing of warnings so the criterion enforces timing more precisely.
suggestion_reasons.Code-Prose Complementarity: All three drafts scored identically because the task didn't call for code, making this criterion inert for this writing type. Kept unchanged — it applies correctly when code is present and shouldn't be removed from the rubric because of one prose-only task.
suggestion_reasons.Narrative-Driven Section Structure: The user ranked the generic draft above the rubric-guided draft overall despite the rubric-guided draft winning on this criterion, which suggests structure alone doesn't override prose tightness — but the criterion is still capturing something real. Kept unchanged.
suggestion_reasons.Reader-Respectful Tone Around Difficulty: The user ranked the generic draft second despite its lower score here, suggesting tone is not the deciding factor when prose economy is lacking. Kept unchanged — the criterion is still valid and the rubric-guided draft earned it.
suggestion_reasons.Prose Economy and Sentence-Level Precisi: The user's primary reason for ranking the generic draft first was that 'every sentence earns its place' and the rubric-guided draft had padding and a quip where a warning should have been — a prose economy concern that no existing criterion captures. Adding this criterion to surface sentence-level tightness as an explicit quality bar.
